perturbation.original_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA, regardless of whether it is identified as confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for information to be 'identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary' to qualify as 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION,' this clause now contradicts the subsequent clauses that rely on this definition. Specifically, clause 3(a) which states the parties agree to treat 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' as confidential loses its meaning, since any information exchanged is now considered confidential, whether explicitly labeled or not, creating an in-text contradiction within the 'Non-Disclosure Agreement' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. In consideration of each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Parties agree to: (a) treat as confidential and to preserve the confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; (b) use any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION solely in connection with the performance of the IRA and for no other purpose; <PAGE> -3- (c) make no disclosures of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any party other than officers and employees of a Party to this IRA; (d) limit access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to those officers and employees having a reasonable need for such INFORMATION and being boUnd by a written obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such INFORMATION; and (e) maintain in confidence any information regarding the nature or scope of any transaction between the Parties, except to the extent such information must be disclosed pursuant to law, and then only after notifying the other Party of such requirement. Any obligation imposed by this paragraph 3 may be waived in writing by a Party as to particular CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and to a particular use or disclosure. Any such waiver will have a one-time effect and will not apply to any subsequent situation regardless of its similarity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' to be identified as such at the time of disclosure. This directly contradicts section 3(a), which states the parties agree to treat 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' as confidential. By removing the identification requirement, any information exchanged becomes confidential, regardless of whether it's labeled or not, rendering the obligation in 3(a) to 'treat as confidential' meaningless and creating a clear in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' type.

perturbation.original_text: Any obligation imposed by this paragraph 3 may be waived in writing by a Party as to particular CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and to a particular use or disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Any obligation imposed by this paragraph 3 may be waived orally by a Party as to particular CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and to a particular use or disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This modification replaces 'in writing' with 'orally'. This contradicts the necessity of written documentation for waiving confidentiality obligations, potentially causing confusion and disputes regarding the enforceability of such waivers. The in-text contradiction arises because, generally, contracts require important waivers to be in writing to prevent misunderstandings and provide clear evidence, and this change introduces an exception without clear guidelines, creating uncertainty within the 'Non-Disclosure Agreement' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. In consideration of each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Parties agree to: (a) treat as confidential and to preserve the confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; (b) use any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION solely in connection with the performance of the IRA and for no other purpose; <PAGE> -3- (c) make no disclosures of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any party other than officers and employees of a Party to this IRA; (d) limit access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to those officers and employees having a reasonable need for such INFORMATION and being boUnd by a written obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such INFORMATION; and (e) maintain in confidence any information regarding the nature or scope of any transaction between the Parties, except to the extent such information must be disclosed pursuant to law, and then only after notifying the other Party of such requirement. Any obligation imposed by this paragraph 3 may be waived orally by a Party as to particular CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and to a particular use or disclosure. Any such waiver will have a one-time effect and will not apply to any subsequent situation regardless of its similarity.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'in writing' to 'orally' directly contradicts the general principle that waivers of confidentiality obligations should be documented in writing to prevent misunderstandings and provide clear evidence. While paragraph 3 outlines the confidentiality obligations, the changed text introduces an exception allowing oral waivers, creating uncertainty within the 'Non-Disclosure Agreement' section and potentially conflicting with the implied requirement of written documentation. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text is in the same location, therefore a change of the 'written' requirement to 'oral' directly causes an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The obligations of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will expire five (5) years from the termination of the JRA.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The obligations of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will never expire.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause stating that confidentiality obligations expire after five years. It removes the expiration date entirely, creating an indefinite obligation. This in-text contradiction makes it unclear whether and when the parties are released from their confidentiality duties. This can cause legal disputes regarding the duration of the confidentiality and obligations within the 'Non-Disclosure Agreement' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. The obligations of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will never expire.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 5-year expiration for obligations, while the changed text removes this expiration, stating the obligations will 'never expire'. This creates a direct contradiction because both statements cannot be true simultaneously. The contradicted text also states 'never expire'. The change directly alters the duration of obligations, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes regarding the enforceable timeframe, thus satisfying the conditions for a strong in-text contradiction under the provided definitions, specifically under 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 8. The Parties understand and agree that no right or license under any patent, patent application, or know-how is granted to any other Party or any other person by this Agreement or by any disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The Parties understand and agree that a limited license under any patent, patent application, or know-how is automatically granted to the receiving Party for internal research purposes only by this Agreement or by any disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly denies the granting of any rights or licenses to the other party regarding patents, applications, or know-how. The modified text introduces a limited license for internal research. This is a direct contradiction, making it unclear whether the receiving party has any right to use the disclosed information for research purposes. This can lead to disputes about the scope of permitted use, specifically in the 'Non-Disclosure Agreement' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The Parties understand and agree that a limited license under any patent, patent application, or know-how is automatically granted to the receiving Party for internal research purposes only by this Agreement or by any disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 8 explicitly states that no rights or licenses are granted. The changed text in Section 8 introduces a limited license for internal research, which directly contradicts the initial statement of no license being granted. Even though the contradiction text is in the same location as the changed text, the change itself creates a clear and direct conflict within the document about the existence of a license, fitting the definition of a structural flaw. This creates uncertainty regarding the use of confidential information, as it's unclear whether any license applies or not.

perturbation.original_text: In addition, all intellectual property, including but not limited to inventions, conceptions, ideas, know-how, discoveries, processes, machines, manufactures, compositions of matter, formulations, processes, biological material, biological methods, or any improvements thereof, whether or not patentable or suitable for other form of exclusive right or legal protection, conceived, made or derived during the course of Work within the Project under this JRA shall be owned by and assigned to the Parties as follows: o All medical uses and inventions that arise out of this JRA shall be owned by Nanolution. o All purification processes for raw halloysite and all non-medical applications that arise out of this JRA shall be owned by NaturalNano.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition, all intellectual property, including but not limited to inventions, conceptions, ideas, know-how, discoveries, processes, machines, manufactures, compositions of matter, formulations, processes, biological material, biological methods, or any improvements thereof, whether or not patentable or suitable for other form of exclusive right or legal protection, conceived, made or derived during the course of Work within the Project under this JRA shall be owned equally by both Parties, regardless of the field of use or application. o All medical uses and inventions that arise out of this JRA shall be owned by Nanolution. o All purification processes for raw halloysite and all non-medical applications that arise out of this JRA shall be owned by NaturalNano.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces the statement that 'all intellectual property... shall be owned equally by both Parties, regardless of the field of use or application' while simultaneously retaining the original stipulations which assign ownership of medical uses/inventions to Nanolution, and purification processes/non-medical applications to NaturalNano. This creates a direct in-text contradiction regarding ownership of the intellectual property. It's unclear whether all IP is jointly owned, or if the specified exceptions apply. This contradiction undermines the JRA by creating uncertainty concerning intellectual property rights within the 'Joint Research Agreement' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Joint Research Agreement ("JRA") is effective as of May 25, 2005 by and between Nanolution, LLC ("Nanolution") and NaturalNano Inc. ("NaturalNano") (also referred to herein as "the Parties"). The parties have agreed to conduct and support joint research in the field of drug delivery utilizing naturally occurring halloysite nanotechnologies ("the Project"). This JRA covers the exchange of ideas and information as well as the performance of experimental, developmental, and/or research work ("the Work") performed under the Project, and the ownership of confidential or proprietary information, including any intellectual property. The term of the Project shall be from May 25, 2005 and shall continue at least until the desired drug delivery technology has been proven commercially viable, or until mutually terminated by both parties. The Work performed as a result of the Project shall be subject to this JRA regardless of where the Work is performed or by whom. In addition, all intellectual property, including but not limited to inventions, conceptions, ideas, know-how, discoveries, processes, machines, manufactures, compositions of matter, formulations, processes, biological material, biological methods, or any improvements thereof, whether or not patentable or suitable for other form of exclusive right or legal protection, conceived, made or derived during the course of Work within the Project under this JRA shall be owned equally by both Parties, regardless of the field of use or application. o All medical uses and inventions that arise out of this JRA shall be owned by Nanolution. o All purification processes for raw halloysite and all non-medical applications that arise out of this JRA shall be owned by NaturalNano.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct in-text contradiction. Initially, the contract specifies ownership of intellectual property (IP) related to medical uses to Nanolution and purification processes/non-medical applications to NaturalNano. The modified text then states that all IP is owned equally by both parties, regardless of use. This directly conflicts with the earlier, specific assignments of IP ownership, creating a strong contradiction within the 'Joint Research Agreement' section. The contradictory text is present in both the 'Changed Text' and 'Contradicted Text' sections. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
